name: Oh My OpenCode lean workflow for WPF testing and UI refactor
overview: Create a low-overhead Oh My OpenCode operating model for a WPF project that minimizes agent count, dispatches, and iteration loops while still covering test work and UI refactor work.

feature.title: Oh My OpenCode lean workflow for WPF testing and UI refactor

content: Deliver a concise but complete markdown guide tailored to the Jvedio WPF repository. The guide should recommend the minimum practical agent topology and explicit token-saving limits.

todolist.content: Archive the previous active feature and switch planning focus to omoc-wpf-lean-workflow
Inspect current WPF UI entry points, style surface, and test layout in Jvedio
Draft the markdown guide with option paths, topology, limits, and repository-specific mapping
Refine the guide in two internal passes and persist the final polished version
Wait for the user to confirm the recommended single-agent path before any execution-oriented handoff

openQuestions: Whether the user wants to stay on the recommended single-agent path or intentionally keep a lightweight explore subagent available.
Whether a later non-planning mode should export the guide to the Desktop as originally requested.

optionPaths.name: Single-agent strict serial workflow
optionPaths.applicability: Best for one-subscription users who want the lowest token spend and the least orchestration overhead.
advantages: Best fit for large WPF XAML and mixed code-behind files
risks: Large features can still bloat context if not sliced carefully
optionPaths.impact: Keep one primary agent only, split testing and UI work into separate features, and allow at most one on-demand explore call per feature.

optionPaths.name: Dual-agent lightweight collaboration
optionPaths.applicability: Use only if repository-wide searching is repeatedly necessary and the user accepts extra token spend.
optionPaths.impact: Keep one main agent plus a tightly capped explore subagent whenever discovery work clearly outweighs the added cost.

archivedReason: Switched active planning focus to unit-test-refactor at the user's request.
